title: Data-subject breach communication timeline tracker (Art.34 / §164.404)
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate risk assessment that drives the Art.34 threshold — that classification is input to this UC.
evidence: Daily search 'data_subject_comm_sla' writes the fan-out state of every Art.34 / §164.404 communication campaign to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.34 high-risk breach communication to data subjects — the UC tracks message dispatch, delivery, and failure rates.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GDPR Art.34 (Breach communication to data subjects) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.39.3: Data-subject breach communication timeline tracker (Art.34 / §164.404).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.39.3' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: When an Art.34 / §164.404 campaign is open and fewer than 95% of in-scope data subjects have a confirmed delivery or bounced-and-retried event 50 days after classification, the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Campaigns that hit the 95% threshold, or individual bounces handled via registered post fall-back, do NOT fire.

spl:
index=comms sourcetype=breach:notification earliest=-90d
| stats count(eval(status="delivered")) as delivered count(eval(status="bounced_handled")) as fallback count as total by campaign_id
| eval ok=(delivered+fallback)/total
| lookup breach_campaigns.csv campaign_id OUTPUT classification classified_at
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(classified_at,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))/86400,0)
| where age_days>=50 AND ok<0.95
| table campaign_id classified_at age_days total delivered fallback ok
description: Measures every open high-risk-breach communication campaign against the statutory clocks (GDPR 'without undue delay', HIPAA 60 days). Campaigns falling behind the completion-rate threshold are surfaced to the DPO for intervention.
value: Data-subject communication is routinely cited in enforcement decisions (Meta, Uber, British Airways); measuring it is the only credible evidence of 'taken reasonable efforts'.
implementation: (1) Ingest campaign events from the messaging platform; (2) join to breach_campaigns.csv with classification timestamps; (3) run daily; (4) escalate via SOAR. Indexes required: `index=comms`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=breach:notification`.
visualization: Per-campaign completion funnel, aging bar chart, single-value '% of campaigns on track'. Key fields: `age_days`, `campaign_id`, `classified_at`, `delivered`, `fallback`, `ok`.
knownFalsePositives: Notification timer breaches during weekend incidents, after-hours teams, or initial scoping.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: See subcategory preamble..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest campaign events from the messaging platform; (2) join to breach_campaigns.csv with classification timestamps; (3) run daily; (4) escalate via SOAR.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=comms sourcetype=breach:notification earliest=-90d
| stats count(eval(status="delivered")) as delivered count(eval(status="bounced_handled")) as fallback count as total by campaign_id
| eval ok=(delivered+fallback)/total
| lookup breach_campaigns.csv campaign_id OUTPUT classification classified_at
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(classified_at,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))/86400,0)
| where age_days>=50 AND ok<0.95
| table campaign_id classified_at age_days total delivered fallback ok
```

Understanding this SPL

**Data-subject breach communication timeline tracker (Art.34 / §164.404)** — Data-subject communication is routinely cited in enforcement decisions (Meta, Uber, British Airways); measuring it is the only credible evidence of 'taken reasonable efforts'.

Documented **Data sources**: See subcategory preamble. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: comms; **sourcetype**: breach:notification. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=comms, sourcetype=breach:notification, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **campaign_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ok** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where age_days>=50 AND ok<0.95` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: age_days, campaign_id, classified_at, delivered, fallback, ok, total.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ES Incident Review queue and the GRC system; verify notification timers and material facts against the official breach and regulator submission record.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Per-campaign completion funnel, aging bar chart, single-value '% of campaigns on track'.
grandmaExplanation: We help you see when incident clocks are tight so you can meet notification duties even after hours.
